files.description: Complete list of plugin-related files with descriptions

items.description: Core plugin SDK types: Plugin, PluginModule, PluginInput, WorkspaceAdapter, Hooks, AuthHook, ProviderHook. Defines the server/CLI plugin contract.
Desktop plugin SDK types: DesktopPluginApi, DesktopPluginModule, DesktopHostSlotMap, DesktopSlotPlugin, DesktopSlotMap. Defines the Electron renderer plugin contract with slot registration.
TUI plugin SDK (635 lines). TuiPluginApi, TuiPluginModule, TuiHostSlotMap, TuiAttention, TuiTheme, TuiCommand, TuiDialog, TuiEventBus, etc.
Tool definition types: ToolContext, ToolDefinition, ToolResult, ToolAttachment, tool() helper function.
BunShell interface types for plugin shell access.
Example server plugin showing tool registration pattern.
Example workspace adapter plugin showing FolderWorkspacePlugin pattern.
Package @opencode-ai/plugin v1.15.12. Exports: '.', './desktop', './tool', './tui'. Dependencies: @opencode-ai/sdk, effect, zod.
DesktopPluginLoader class — reads plugin config from IPC, dynamically imports modules, constructs DesktopPluginApi, calls plugin.desktop(). Manages slot registration and lifecycle disposal.
DesktopPluginProvider, DesktopSlotRenderer, useDesktopSlot — SolidJS context provider for plugin slot system. Wraps the app in a context that manages slot component registration/render.
Plugin Manager dialog (DialogManagePlugins). Lists installed plugins with enable/disable toggle, remove button, and install form for npm/file sources.
Standalone Install Plugin dialog (DialogInstallPlugin). Supports npm package or file path installation.
Desktop app entry point. Wraps app in DesktopPluginProvider, handles 'open-plugin-manager' menu command via dynamic import of dialog-manage-plugins.
Internationalization strings for plugin manager UI (21 entries: desktop.plugin.*).
Single source of truth for all IPC channel names. Plugin-related: GET_DESKTOP_PLUGIN_CONFIG, SET_DESKTOP_PLUGIN_CONFIG (handle group), DESKTOP_PLUGIN_CONFIG (store group).
IPC handler registration (registerIpcHandlers). GET_DESKTOP_PLUGIN_CONFIG reads from electron-store 'desktop-plugin-config' and validates. SET_DESKTOP_PLUGIN_CONFIG writes with serializedWrite queue.
validateAndFilterPluginConfigs — validates PluginConfigEntry shape, drops invalid entries.
PluginConfigEntry type definition and ElectronAPI interface methods (getDesktopPluginConfig, setDesktopPluginConfig).
contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld preload script. Wires getDesktopPluginConfig/setDesktopPluginConfig to ipcRenderer.invoke calls.

OpenCode server-side plugin service (Effect-based). Plugin.Service interface, internal plugins (OpenAI Codex, GitHub Copilot, Azure, xAI, Cloudflare, DigitalOcean, GitLab, Poe). Manages plugin lifecycle: trigger(), list(), init().

ToolRegistry service. Imports ToolContext and ToolDefinition from @opencode-ai/plugin. fromPlugin() converts plugin tool definitions into Tool.Def instances. Built-in tool list includes plugin tool registration.
Desktop menu definition. Help menu includes 'Plugin Manager' item with command 'open-plugin-manager', accelerator Cmd+Shift+P.

ipc_patterns.description: Every usage of ipcRenderer/ipcMain in plugin code, with exact code snippets

patterns.mechanism: ipcMain.handle — reads/writes to electron-store, validates data, returns filtered results

patterns.code: const result = (await window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig?.().catch(() => undefined)) ?? { configs: [] as PluginConfigEntry[], dropped: 0 }
patterns.mechanism: Plugin loader calls window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig (bridged through preload) to get plugin configs
Plugin manager dialog reads current configs, toggles/deletes entries, writes back via preload bridge

patterns.direction: renderer install dialog reads/writes plugin configs
patterns.mechanism: Install dialog reads config, checks for duplicates, adds new entry, writes back

channel_names.description: All IPC channel names used for plugin communication

channels.usage: electron-store namespace key for persisting plugin configuration

types.description: All plugin-related interfaces/types found in the codebase

types: Plugin = (input: PluginInput, options?: PluginOptions) => Promise<Hooks>
PluginModule = { id?: string; server: Plugin; tui?: never; desktop?: never }
PluginInput = { hooks: Hooks; config: Config; project?: Project; client: SDKClient; experimental_workspace: WorkspaceApi }
PluginOptions = Record<string, unknown>
Hooks { tool: Record<string, ToolDefinition>; auth: Record<string, AuthHook>; provider: Record<string, ProviderHook>; hook: { ... } }
WorkspaceAdapter = { name, description, configure, create, remove, target }
AuthHook = { name: string; description: string; oauth: ..., authenticate: ..., clear: ..., refresh: ... }
ProviderHook = { ... }
ToolContext = { sessionID, messageID, agent, directory, worktree, abort, metadata, ask }
ToolDefinition = ReturnType<typeof tool>
ToolResult = string | { title?, output, metadata?, attachments? }
DesktopHostSlotMap = { window_titlebar: {}; window_titlebar_left: {}; sidebar_content: {}; sidebar_footer: {} }
DesktopSlotMap<Slots> = DesktopHostSlotMap & Slots
DesktopSlotPlugin<Slots> = { slots: { [Name in keyof (DesktopHostSlotMap & Slots)]?: Component<...> } }
DesktopPluginApi = { slots: { register(slotPlugin): () => void }; store: { get, set }; lifecycle: { onDispose } }
DesktopPlugin = (api: DesktopPluginApi) => Promise<void>
DesktopPluginModule = { id?: string; desktop: DesktopPlugin; server?: never; tui?: never }
TuiPluginApi = { renderer, slots, plugins: { list, activate, deactivate, add, install }, lifecycle }
TuiPluginModule = { id?: string; tui: TuiPlugin; server?: never }
TuiPlugin = (api, options, meta) => Promise<void>
TuiHostSlotMap = { route_header, route_content, route_footer, sidebar_header, sidebar_content, sidebar_footer, ... }
TuiSlotPlugin<Slots> = { ... }
TuiCommand = { id, name, description, keys?, mode?, ... }
PluginConfigEntry = { name: string; path: string; enabled: boolean; config?: Record<string, unknown> }
SlotName = keyof DesktopHostSlotMap
SlotRegistry = Map<SlotName, Component<{}>>
DesktopPluginContextValue = { slots: SlotRegistry; registerSlot: (name, component) => () => void }
Interface = { trigger: (name, input) => Promise<void>; list: () => PluginMeta[]; init: Effect<void> }
Service = Context.Service<Service, Interface>('@opencode/Plugin')
State = { hooks: Hooks; loaded: PluginMeta[] }

lifecycle.description: How plugins are loaded, registered, communicate, and torn down

server_plugin_lifecycle.loading: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts: internalPlugins() builds a list of built-in PluginInstances (Codex, Copilot, Azure, xAI, Cloudflare, DigitalOcean, GitLab, Poe) plus legacy plugins from config. Each is called as Plugin(input, options) returning Hooks.
server_plugin_lifecycle.registration: Plugins return Hooks objects with tool, auth, provider, and hook registrations. These are fused into the runtime via Plugin.Service.trigger().
server_plugin_lifecycle.communication: No IPC involved — server plugins run in-process in the opencode CLI/sidecar. They communicate via Effect services and the Hooks interface.
server_plugin_lifecycle.teardown: Plugin.Service manages lifecycle through Effect's Scope/ManagedRuntime. The plugin layer gets disposed when the sidecar session ends.
server_plugin_lifecycle.third_party: Third-party plugin packages (opencode-gitlab-auth, opencode-poe-auth) are imported as npm dependencies.

loading: (1) On mount, DesktopPluginProvider creates a DesktopPluginLoader(registerSlot).
(2) DesktopPluginLoader.loadAll() calls ensureConfigs() which invokes window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig() (IPC to main process).
(3) Main process reads 'desktop-plugin-config' from electron-store, validates/filters via validateAndFilterPluginConfigs(), returns { configs, dropped }.
(4) Loader iterates enabled configs, calls loadEntry() which does dynamic import(entry.path).
(5) Imported module expected to export DesktopPluginModule with a desktop() function.
(6) DesktopPluginApi is constructed with slots.register, store.get/set, lifecycle.onDispose. Plugin module.desktop(api) is called.
desktop_plugin_lifecycle.slot_registration: Plugins call api.slots.register(slotPlugin) with JSX components. The loader wires them into DesktopSlotRenderer via the registerSlot function passed from DesktopPluginProvider.
desktop_plugin_lifecycle.communication: Desktop plugins run in the Electron renderer process. They communicate with main process indirectly via the preload bridge (window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig/setDesktopPluginConfig). No custom plugin IPC channels exist beyond the config get/set.
desktop_plugin_lifecycle.teardown: DesktopPluginLoader.dispose() calls all onDispose callbacks, unregisters all slot components, clears loaded set. Called via onCleanup in DesktopPluginProvider.
desktop_plugin_lifecycle.third_party_installation: Users add plugins via Plugin Manager dialog (npm package or file path). Config stored in electron-store 'desktop-plugin-config'. Requires app restart to load new plugins (no hot-reload mechanism).

ui_slot_render.DesktopSlotRenderer: Looks up component by slot name in the slot registry Map. If found, renders it inside an ErrorBoundary. If not found, renders null.
ui_slot_render.useDesktopSlot: SolidJS hook. On mount, registers a component at a named slot. On cleanup (dispose), unregisters it.

items.name: IPC Channel Registry
items.description: Single source of truth for all IPC channel names. Four groups: handle (two-way RPC), send (fire-and-forget), push (main→renderer push), store (electron-store namespaces). All plugin IPC is in the 'handle' group (RPC pattern).

items.name: Preload Bridge (contextBridge)
items.description: contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld creates the window.api object. This is the only bridge between renderer (SolidJS) and main (Node.js/Electron). All IPC goes through this typed bridge interface (ElectronAPI type).

items.name: electron-store
items.description: Persistence layer using electron-store. Plugin config stored under namespace 'desktop-plugin-config' with key 'configs'. All writes go through serializedWrite queue to prevent concurrent store corruption.

items.name: IPC Validation Layer
items.description: Shared validation functions for agent configs, MCP servers, and plugin configs. validateAndFilterPluginConfigs() validates name, path, enabled fields, drops invalid entries, returns count of dropped items.

items.name: DesktopPluginApi (Plugin SDK Abstraction)
items.description: The DesktopPluginApi type provides an abstracted interface for plugins: slot registration, scoped key-value store, and lifecycle hooks. This isolates plugins from direct IPC or Electron API access.

items.name: Slot System (Composition Abstraction)
items.description: Slot-based UI composition: desktop host defines named slots (window_titlebar, sidebar_content, sidebar_footer, window_titlebar_left), plugins register SolidJS components, renderer looks up and renders them.

items.name: Effect-based Plugin Service
items.description: The opencode CLI/server side uses Effect's Context/Service/Layer system to manage plugins as a service dependency. Internal plugins are fused through Layer.provide chains.

smoking_guns.description: Potential issues, risks, and findings discovered

items.summary: Desktop plugins are renderer-only — no main process plugin system
items.detail: DesktopPluginLoader runs entirely in Electron renderer. Plugins cannot register main-process IPC handlers, native menus, or system tray. All plugin capabilities are limited to SolidJS component slots and a scoped Map store.

Plugins are loaded once on mount. Adding/removing via the Plugin Manager dialog requires app restart. The dialog only updates the persisted config — the currently loaded plugins in DesktopPluginLoader are not refreshed.

items.summary: Plugin config is not scoped by project or environment
items.detail: All plugin configs are stored globally in electron-store under a single 'desktop-plugin-config' key. No per-project or per-environment plugin configurations exist.

items.summary: Only two IPC channels for the entire plugin system
items.detail: GET_DESKTOP_PLUGIN_CONFIG and SET_DESKTOP_PLUGIN_CONFIG are the only plugin-specific IPC channels. Desktop plugins cannot define custom IPC handlers — they are limited to the slot/per-store API provided by DesktopPluginApi.

The plugin 'path' in config is passed directly to dynamic import(). There's no path resolution, sandboxing, or dependency management for npm-installed plugins vs file-path plugins. Both work identically.

items.summary: Server plugins and desktop plugins are entirely separate systems
items.detail: The server/CLI plugin system (packages/opencode/src/plugin/) uses Effect and runs in the sidecar. The desktop plugin system (packages/desktop/src/renderer/) uses SolidJS slots and runs in the renderer. They share no code, no IPC, and no lifecycle — they are two independent plugin architectures.
